name: Palworld
author: ops@refx.example
description: Pocketpair's creature-collection survival crafting game. SteamCMD-installed dedicated server with RCON support.
startupDetect: Setting breakpad minidump|Running Palworld dedicated server

installScript.script:
#!/bin/bash
# ReFx steamcmd shim — the steamcmd:debian/proton install image ships no
# `steamcmd` on PATH. Bootstrap SteamCMD and expose it on PATH so the egg's
# `steamcmd ...` calls resolve (otherwise the game server never downloads).
if ! command -v steamcmd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  mkdir -p /mnt/server/steamcmd
  curl -sSL https://steamcdn-a.akamaihd.net/client/installer/steamcmd_linux.tar.gz | tar zxf - -C /mnt/server/steamcmd || { echo "ERROR: failed to fetch steamcmd"; exit 1; }
  chmod +x /mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh /mnt/server/steamcmd/linux32/steamcmd 2>/dev/null || true
  printf '#!/bin/sh\nexec /mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh "$@"\n' > /usr/local/bin/steamcmd
  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/steamcmd
fi
set -euo pipefail
cd /mnt/server
steamcmd +force_install_dir /mnt/server +login anonymous +app_update 2394010 validate +quit
mkdir -p /mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer
chmod +x PalServer.sh || true

# Seed the live config from the game's reference file so the customer's first
# file-manager visit shows a fully-populated OptionSettings line, not an empty
# file. NEVER overwrites an existing config (reinstall-safe).
INI=/mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini
if ! grep -q 'OptionSettings=(' "$INI" 2>/dev/null && [ -f /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini ]; then
  cp /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini "$INI"
  echo 'Seeded PalWorldSettings.ini from DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini.'
fi

# Drop the ReFx launcher (see its header for why gameplay settings are synced
# into the ini instead of passed as CLI flags).
cat > /mnt/server/refx-palworld-run.sh <<'RUNEOF'
#!/bin/bash
# ReFx launcher for Palworld.
#
# PalWorldSettings.ini is the source of truth for world settings. Palworld
# silently prefers command-line flags over the ini, so the old startup passed
# -servername/-AdminPassword/... and made customer ini edits look ignored
# ("no admin password set" even though the ini had one). Instead, the panel's
# Startup-tab variables own exactly these OptionSettings keys and are written
# INTO the ini on each boot:
#   ServerName, ServerPlayerMaxNum, RCONEnabled, RCONPort  (always)
#   AdminPassword                                          (only when set —
#                                                           never blanked)
#   PublicPort                                             (follows the
#                                                           allocated port)
# Every other key belongs to the customer via the file manager.
set -u
cd /home/container 2>/dev/null || cd "$(dirname "$0")"

INI_DIR="Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer"
INI="${INI_DIR}/PalWorldSettings.ini"
mkdir -p "${INI_DIR}"

# First boot / wiped config: seed a fully-populated OptionSettings line from
# the game's reference file so customers never start from an empty tuple (the
# classic Palworld footgun: an empty file means every setting is a default).
if ! grep -q 'OptionSettings=(' "${INI}" 2>/dev/null; then
  if [ -f DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini ]; then
    cp DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini "${INI}"
    echo "[refx] seeded ${INI} from DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini"
  else
    printf '[/Script/Pal.PalGameWorldSettings]\nOptionSettings=()\n' > "${INI}"
    echo "[refx] WARNING: DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini missing; wrote an empty OptionSettings"
  fi
fi

# ---- OptionSettings tuple editing ------------------------------------------
# Values are sanitized so a customer-supplied value can never corrupt the
# single-line tuple: quoted strings drop quotes + control chars, numbers keep
# digits only, booleans normalize to True/False. The sed delimiter is \x01,
# which the sanitizers guarantee never appears in a value.
D=$'\x01'
sstr()  { printf '%s' "$1" | tr -d '"[:cntrl:]'; }
snum()  { printf '%s' "$1" | tr -cd '0-9'; }
sbool() { case "$(printf '%s' "$1" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')" in
            1|true|yes|on) printf 'True' ;; *) printf 'False' ;; esac; }
# Escape sed-replacement metacharacters (backslash and ampersand).
esc()   { printf '%s' "$1" | sed -e 's/[\\&]/\\&/g'; }

# set_opt KEY VALUE-TOKEN — replace the key inside OptionSettings=(...) or
# insert it if absent. VALUE-TOKEN arrives already quoted/sanitized.
set_opt() {
  local key="$1" rep
  rep="$(esc "${key}=$2")"
  if grep -Eq "[(,][[:space:]]*${key}=" "${INI}"; then
    sed -i -E "s${D}([(,][[:space:]]*)${key}=(\"[^\"]*\"|[^,)]*)${D}\1${rep}${D}" "${INI}"
  elif grep -q 'OptionSettings=()' "${INI}"; then
    sed -i "s${D}OptionSettings=()${D}OptionSettings=(${rep})${D}" "${INI}"
  else
    sed -i "s${D}OptionSettings=(${D}OptionSettings=(${rep},${D}" "${INI}"
  fi
}

if [ -n "${SERVER_NAME:-}" ]; then
  set_opt ServerName "\"$(sstr "${SERVER_NAME}")\""
fi
mp="$(snum "${MAX_PLAYERS:-}")"
if [ -n "${mp}" ]; then
  set_opt ServerPlayerMaxNum "${mp}"
fi
if [ -n "${ADMIN_PASS:-}" ]; then
  set_opt AdminPassword "\"$(sstr "${ADMIN_PASS}")\""
else
  echo "[refx] AdminPassword: Startup-tab variable is unset — leaving the value in PalWorldSettings.ini untouched"
fi
set_opt RCONEnabled "$(sbool "${RCON_ENABLED:-true}")"
rp="$(snum "${RCON_PORT:-}")"
if [ -n "${rp}" ]; then
  set_opt RCONPort "${rp}"
fi
gp="$(snum "${SERVER_PORT:-8211}")"
if [ -n "${gp}" ]; then
  set_opt PublicPort "${gp}"
fi

echo "[refx] PalWorldSettings.ini synced — panel-managed keys: ServerName, ServerPlayerMaxNum, RCONEnabled, RCONPort, PublicPort${ADMIN_PASS:+, AdminPassword}. All other settings are read from the ini as you saved it."

exec ./PalServer.sh -port="${gp:-8211}" -useperfthreads -NoAsyncLoadingThread -UseMultithreadForDS
RUNEOF
chmod +x /mnt/server/refx-palworld-run.sh
echo 'Palworld install complete.'

variables.description: Name shown in the in-game server browser. Panel-managed: written into PalWorldSettings.ini (ServerName) on every boot.
Maximum concurrent players (hard cap of 32). Panel-managed: written into PalWorldSettings.ini (ServerPlayerMaxNum) on every boot.

Password for in-game admin commands (/AdminPassword) and RCON. When set, it is written into PalWorldSettings.ini (AdminPassword) on every boot; leave blank to manage AdminPassword directly in the ini via the file manager.
Enable the remote console interface. Panel-managed: written into PalWorldSettings.ini (RCONEnabled) on every boot.
TCP port for the RCON interface (allocated by the panel and written into PalWorldSettings.ini on every boot).

longDescription: Co-op creature-collecting survival. Spin up a Palworld dedicated server for you and your friends in minutes.
